message:
style: apply Clippy lint fixes across 181 files (clone_on_copy, uninlined_format_args, misc idioms)

Codebase-wide mechanical cleanup resolving the following Clippy lints
with zero semantic impact:

- clone_on_copy: Remove redundant `.clone()` calls on Copy types in
  test and production code (~160 files). Values are now used directly
  instead of explicitly cloned when the type already implements Copy.

- uninlined_format_args: Inline format arguments in format!/assert!
  macros (e.g., `format!("{}", x)` → `format!("{x}")`) per the
  uninlined_format_args lint (~30 files).

- bool_assert_comparison: Replace `assert_eq!(x, true)` with
  `assert!(x)` and `assert_eq!(x, false)` with `assert!(!x)`.

- single_char_pattern: Use char literals instead of single-char
  string literals in string search methods (e.g., `contains("5")` →
  `contains('5')`).

- default_constructed_unit_structs: Replace `UnitStruct::default()`
  with direct `UnitStruct` construction for zero-field structs
  (BytesCodec, AlwaysRetry, NeverRetry, IdentityCodec, FailFast,
  CollectAll, LoadShedLayer, NoopInterceptor, IdentityCompressor).

- Use `std::io::Error::other()` instead of
  `std::io::Error::new(ErrorKind::Other, ...)` (modernized API).

- Replace `len() > 0` with `!is_empty()` per the len_zero lint.

Touches channels, codecs, combinators, distributed, gRPC, HTTP, lab,
messaging, net, obligations, observability, plan, raptorq (pipeline/
proof/rfc6330/systematic only), records, runtime, security, server,
service, signals, sync, time, trace, transport, types, util, web,
and test modules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
